goal: Make the committed BPK governance manifest check stable on Windows by forcing LF checkout for the generated manifest artifact.
scope_out: Do not change manifest semantics, generator logic, tests, runtime routes, provider behavior, write behavior, deployment configuration, package files, secrets, or environment variables.
claims: The BPK governance manifest check fails on Windows because the working-tree file is CRLF while the generator emits LF.
Forcing LF checkout for builder/data/bpk-governance-manifest.json mirrors the existing builder-repo-index convention.
The generated manifest content remains semantically unchanged.
assumptions: Git may check out text files as CRLF on Windows when no eol rule is present.
The manifest generator should remain byte-exact rather than weakening its check.
dod: The manifest artifact is renormalized without semantic content changes.
Focused governance manifest test, typecheck, task-lock verify, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: Manifest semantics, generator code, tests, runtime, provider, write, DB, auth, deploy, package, workflow, or environment changes become necessary.
prior_task_findings: Post-BPK-226 verification found live Bluepilot healthy on 05ec630c, typecheck green, and npm test red only because bpk-governance-manifest.json was checked out as CRLF on Windows while the generator emits LF.
